items.summary_en: Three tips to improve dialect recognition for in-car voice AI, plus FAQ for better user experience.
items.why_zh: 解决方言识别痛点，提升车载AI用户体验。
items.why_en: Addresses dialect recognition challenges, improving in-car AI user experience.

items.title_zh: 世界杯仅剩6队，AI冠军预测还剩几支？5票巴西已出局，算法名单大洗牌
items.title_en: World Cup: Only 6 Teams Left, AI Predictions Reshuffled
items.summary_zh: 世界杯仅剩6队，AI冠军预测还剩几支？5票巴西已出局，算法名单大洗牌 新浪财经
items.summary_en: With only 6 teams left in the World Cup, AI predictions have been reshuffled after Brazil's elimination, sparking debate on AI reliability.
items.why_zh: 反映AI预测在动态赛事中的局限性，引发对算法可靠性的思考。
items.why_en: Reflects limitations of AI predictions in dynamic events, prompting reliability concerns.

items.title_zh: 面向主动企业智能体的上下文图
items.title_en: Context Graphs for Proactive Enterprise Agents
items.summary_zh: 本文提出上下文图（Context Graph），一种实时关系数据结构，用于建模企业实体、关系及状态变化。基于此图，系统可主动向员工推送相关信息，实现从被动查询到主动服务的转变，有望显著提升企业生产力。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07721v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and agentic frameworks have advanced enterprise AI considerably, yet agents remain fundamentally reactive: they wait for a human query before acting. This paper argues that genuine enterprise productivity gains require proactive agents: systems that surface relevant, actionable information to workers before they ask. We propose the Context Graph, a live relational data structure that models enterprise entities, their relationships, and state transitions over time. Built on this graph, we define a Delta Detecti…
items.why_zh: 推动企业AI从被动响应向主动服务演进，提升工作效率。
items.why_en: Drives enterprise AI from reactive to proactive, enhancing work efficiency.

items.title_zh: Infinity-Parser2技术报告：多模态文档解析
items.title_en: Infinity-Parser2 Technical Report
items.summary_zh: 本文介绍Infinity-Parser2，一个大型多模态模型，结合可控数据合成管道与多任务强化学习，实现端到端文档解析。团队开源了包含500万样本的双语（中/英）数据集Infinity-Doc2-5M，覆盖多种文档类型，解决了标注语料稀缺问题。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07836v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Infinity-Parser2, a large multimodal model that couples a controllable data-synthesis pipeline with multi-task reinforcement learning for end-to-end document parsing, addressing the persistent scarcity of faithfully annotated parsing corpora. Our contributions are threefold. First, we build a scalable synthesis engine, pairing a controllable rendering framework with an iterative refinement loop, and use it to construct and open-source Infinity-Doc2-5M: a 5-million-sample bilingual (Chinese/English) corpus spanning diverse document type…
items.why_zh: 开源大规模标注数据集，推动文档解析领域发展。
items.why_en: Open-sourcing large annotated dataset advances document parsing research.

items.title_zh: 基于sEMG信号的图神经网络实时手势识别
items.title_en: A Graph Neural Network Model for Real-Time Gesture Recognition Based on sEMG Signals
items.summary_zh: 本文提出一种基于表面肌电信号（sEMG）的图神经网络方法，用于实时手势识别。通过构建前臂肌肉激活模式的图网络，算法能够准确识别手势，为高级假肢和增强现实提供控制支持。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07850v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For seemless control of advanced hand prostheses and augmented reality, accurate and immediate hand gestures recognition is essential. Surface electromyography (sEMG) signals obtained from the forearm are commonly employed for this purpose. In this paper, we present a novel approach for sEMG representation that utilizes graph networks which contain information about muscle activation patterns in the forearm. Based on these graph networks, we have developed a machine learning algorithm capable of real-time hand gesture recognition using a graph ne…
items.why_zh: 提升假肢和AR交互的实时性与准确性。
items.why_en: Improves real-time accuracy for prosthetics and AR interaction.

items.title_zh: 智能体AI与检索增强模型在直通核保中的应用
items.title_en: Agentic AI and Retrieval-Augmented Models in Straight-Through Underwriting
items.summary_zh: 本文探讨AI如何重塑精算实践，特别是通过大语言模型、检索增强生成（RAG）和多智能体系统支持直通核保。这些技术能够处理非结构化文档和异构数据，提高核保的透明度和效率。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07858v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is beginning to reshape actuarial practice, particularly in domains that require reasoning over unstructured documents, heterogeneous data sources, and regulated decision workflows. Actuaries now face a design space that ranges from traditional rule-based automation to large language models (LLMs), retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and multi-agent ``agentic'' systems that plan, retrieve, call tools, and reflect. This paper examines how these emerging architectures can support actuarial priorities such as transpare…
items.why_zh: 推动保险核保自动化与智能化，提升行业效率。
items.why_en: Advances automation and intelligence in insurance underwriting, boosting efficiency.

items.title_zh: 反馈操纵正则化：实现离线智能体模仿学习对齐
items.title_en: Feedback Manipulation Regularization: Enabling Offline Agent Alignment for Imitation Learning
items.summary_zh: 强化学习研究日益聚焦于对齐问题，即确保智能体学习符合人类价值观的行为。人类演示和反馈对于对齐至关重要，但现有方法主要采用多阶段流水线，将这两种信号结合用于语言生成的上下文赌博机框架。然而，很少有工作探索如何将这些互补输入作为更丰富的互连信号，用于完全序列决策环境中的单阶段离线训练。本文提出反馈操纵正则化方法，旨在利用人类反馈和演示的联合信号，在离线设置下实现智能体对齐，从而提升模仿学习的效果。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07859v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) research has increasingly shifted focus towards alignment, ensuring agents learn behaviors adhering to human values. While human demonstrations and feedback have proven crucial for alignment, existing approaches predominantly combine these signals using multi-stage pipelines designed for the contextual bandit framing of language generation. Yet little work explores how these complementary inputs can serve as a richer, interconnected signal for single-stage offline training in fully sequential decision-making environmen…
items.why_zh: 该方法首次将人类反馈和演示作为互连信号用于离线单阶段训练，有望提升智能体在复杂序列决策中的对齐效果。
items.why_en: This method first uses human feedback and demonstrations as an interconnected signal for single-stage offline training, potentially improving agent alignment in complex sequential decision-making.

items.title_zh: 尼日利亚机械：一个低资源工业数据集及领域推理层
items.title_en: Nigeria Machinery: A Low-Resource Industrial Dataset with a Domain-Grounded Reasoning Layer
items.summary_zh: 关于非洲经济体工业机械的公开、模型就绪数据相对较少，这给定量分析或训练基于该领域的数值任务语言模型带来困难。本文发布了两项资源：一是尼日利亚机械使用与故障数据集，包含2006年至2025年间尼日利亚制造业和油气行业的89条机器级记录，涵盖28个指标，每条记录均注明公开来源并附有编码手册；二是提出一种方法，为数据集添加领域推理层，以支持更深入的数值推理任务。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07883v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: There is relatively little, public, and model-ready data on industrial machinery for African economies. This makes it hard to do quantitative analysis or to train language models on numeric tasks grounded in that setting. We release two things to help with part of this problem. The first is the Nigeria Machinery Usage and Failures Dataset: 89 machine-level records across 28 indicators, covering Nigeria's manufacturing and oil and gas sectors from 2006 to 2025. Every record names a public source and is decoded by a codebook. The second is a method…
items.why_zh: 该数据集填补了非洲工业机械领域公开数据的空白，为低资源场景下的数值推理和语言模型应用提供了基础。
items.why_en: This dataset fills a gap in public industrial machinery data for African economies, enabling quantitative analysis and language model training in low-resource settings.

items.title_zh: 具体化命题提示解决大语言模型中的组合-知识二分法
items.title_en: Concretized Proposition Prompting Resolves Composition-Knowledge Dichotomy in Large Language Models
items.summary_zh: 大语言模型常在组合性与知识性之间难以平衡，本文将其定义为组合-知识二分法。为解决此问题，提出具体化命题提示（CPP）框架，该框架明确将与问题相关的命题具体化。实验结果表明，CPP显著提升了推理性能，尤其在需要精确知识的医学基准测试中表现突出，同时在强调演绎推理的数学基准测试中也具有竞争力。额外实验揭示了CPP的有效性机制。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08018v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs often struggle to balance compositionality with knowledgeability, a challenge we define as Composition-Knowledge Dichotomy. To address this, we propose Concretized Proposition Prompting (CPP), a framework that explicitly concretizes propositions relevant to questions. The results demonstrate that CPP significantly enhances reasoning performance, particularly in medical benchmarks where precise knowledge is paramount, while being competitive on math benchmarks where deductive reasoning is prioritized. Additional experiments reveal that CPP is…
items.why_zh: CPP框架有效平衡了大语言模型的组合性与知识性，为提升复杂推理能力提供了新思路。
items.why_en: CPP effectively balances compositionality and knowledgeability in LLMs, offering a new approach to enhance complex reasoning.

items.title_zh: CausalDS：数据科学智能体因果推理基准测试
items.title_en: CausalDS: Benchmarking Causal Reasoning in Data-Science Agents
items.summary_zh: 大语言模型越来越多地作为集成数据科学智能体，结合抽象推理和高级工具使用。然而，现有基准测试要么是符号因果推理基准（缺乏真实数据分析），要么是数据分析基准（缺乏因果数据生成结构）。此外，现有因果评估数据集通常局限于来自现有来源的精选示例，多样性来自有限的模板化变体而非系统生成。本文提出CausalDS基准，旨在系统评估数据科学智能体在真实数据分析场景中的因果推理能力。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08093v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly act as integrated data-science agents, combining abstract reasoning with advanced tool use. Yet the relevant benchmark landscape largely divides into symbolic causal reasoning benchmarks without realistic data analysis or data analysis benchmarks without a principled causal data-generating structure. Furthermore, existing causal evaluation datasets are often restricted to curated examples from existing sources, with diversity coming from limited templatized variations rather than from systematic generatio…
items.why_zh: 该基准填补了数据科学智能体因果推理评估的空白，为开发更智能的数据分析系统提供了标准。
items.why_en: This benchmark fills a gap in evaluating causal reasoning in data-science agents, providing a standard for developing more intelligent data analysis systems.

items.title_zh: ASMR：船舶维护报告编写的智能模式生成
items.title_en: ASMR: Agentic Schema Generation for Ship Maintenance Report Writing
items.summary_zh: 本文研究自动模式生成问题：给定一组历史船舶维护和操作报告（涵盖多种表格类别），自动发现紧凑且信息丰富的模式，以捕捉每种报告类型的基本信息需求。为此，提出ASMR，一个模块化智能体框架，包含两个专门智能体：字段生成智能体从历史叙述中提取语义概念，并通过自适应多粒度方法生成候选模式字段；另一个智能体负责优化和整合。该方法旨在提高报告编写的效率和准确性。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08177v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this paper, we study the automatic schema generation problem: given a collection of historical ship maintenance and operational reports across multiple form categories, automatically discover compact and informative schemas that capture the essential information requirements of each report type. To address this challenge, we propose ASMR, a modular agentic framework consisting of two specialized agents. A Field Generation Agent extracts semantic concepts from historical narratives and generates candidate schema fields through adaptive multi-gr…
items.why_zh: 该框架自动化了船舶维护报告的模式生成，有望大幅提升工业文档编写的效率与质量。
items.why_en: This framework automates schema generation for ship maintenance reports, potentially improving efficiency and quality in industrial documentation.

items.title_zh: 慢思考与主动感知的第一性原理理论
items.title_en: A First-Principles Theory of Slow Thinking and Active Perception
items.summary_zh: 作为认知功能第一性原理建模系列的一部分，本文尝试为思考和感知提供数学形式化。它正式推导了慢思考或更一般的主动感知，并涵盖了慢思考大语言模型的设计、训练和推理。起点是可观测空间和潜在空间上概率分布的提升与投影，目标是用简单函数族（如神经网络）表示复杂数据分布。该理论为理解智能系统的认知过程提供了新的数学框架。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08196v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As part of a series on first-principles modeling of cognitive functions, this paper attempts to provide a mathematical formulation of thinking and perception. It formally derives slow thinking or more generally, active perception, and encompasses the design, training and inference of slow thinking large language models. Our starting point is the lifting and projection of probability distributions on the observable and latent spaces, with the objective of representing complex data distributions by simple function families such as neural networks.…
items.why_zh: 该理论为慢思考大语言模型提供了数学基础，有助于推动更接近人类认知的智能系统发展。
items.why_en: This theory provides a mathematical foundation for slow-thinking LLMs, advancing the development of intelligent systems closer to human cognition.

items.title_zh: 玩转ZendoWorld：挑战AI智能体的主动视觉概念归纳
items.title_en: Playing ZendoWorld: Challenging AI Agents on Active Visual Concept Induction
items.summary_zh: 构建智能系统的一个核心挑战是使智能体能够联合感知复杂输入、形成关于隐藏模式的假设，并设计信息性实验来测试它们。为研究此问题，本文提出ZendoWorld，一个受控交互环境，智能体必须推断关于视觉游戏观察的逻辑规则，通过提出新场景获取信息，并根据游戏环境反馈完善假设。评估了多种智能体，包括纯VLM推理、贝叶斯粒子滤波等，以测试其主动视觉概念归纳能力。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A central challenge in building intelligent systems is enabling agents to jointly perceive complex inputs, form hypotheses about hidden patterns, and design informative experiments to test them. To study this problem, we propose ZendoWorld, a controlled interactive environment in which agents must infer a logical rule about visual game observations, acquire information by proposing new scenes, and refine their hypotheses based on feedback from the game environment. We evaluate several agents spanning pure VLM reasoning, Bayesian particle filterin…
items.why_zh: 该环境为评估智能体的主动视觉推理和假设检验能力提供了标准化测试平台。
items.why_en: This environment provides a standardized testbed for evaluating agents' active visual reasoning and hypothesis testing capabilities.

items.title_zh: MentalHospital：评估精神病学临床会诊的虚拟环境
items.title_en: MentalHospital: A Virtual Environment for Evaluating Psychiatric Clinical Encounters
items.summary_zh: 大语言模型在孤立的精神病学任务（如对话、诊断和治疗计划）上表现强劲，但现有基准很少模拟完整的精神病学临床会诊。本文介绍MentalHospital，一个用于基于LLM的精神病学临床会诊的虚拟评估环境。MentalHospital实例化了主观访谈、客观检查、诊断评估和治疗计划（S.O.A.P.）工作流程，使用由技能增强的标准化患者构建，以模拟真实临床场景。该环境旨在全面评估LLM在精神病学领域的综合能力。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08257v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown strong performance on isolated psychiatric tasks, including dialogue, diagnosis, and treatment planning, yet existing benchmarks rarely simulate complete psychiatric clinical encounters. We introduce $\textbf{MentalHospital}$, a virtual evaluation environment for LLM-based psychiatric clinical encounters. MentalHospital instantiates the Subjective Interviewing, Objective Examination, Diagnostic Assessment, and Treatment Planning (S.O.A.P.) workflow, using skill-augmented standardized patients constructed fr…
items.why_zh: 该环境首次模拟完整的精神病学临床会诊流程，为评估LLM在心理健康领域的应用提供了更真实的基准。
items.why_en: This environment simulates complete psychiatric clinical encounters for the first time, providing a more realistic benchmark for evaluating LLMs in mental health.

items.title_zh: PredicateLongBench：理解长上下文任务的难度轴
items.title_en: Understanding Axes of Difficulty For Long Context Tasks Via PredicateLongBench
items.summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）在长上下文能力上快速提升，催生了一系列评估基准。然而，现有评估（如大海捞针测试、多跳推理和摘要任务）主要衡量平均性能，许多已饱和或缺乏鲁棒性。尤其缺乏一种系统方法来探测模型在不同难度轴上的表现。为此，研究者提出PredicateLongBench，旨在填补这一空白，通过定义多个难度维度来系统评估LLM的长上下文处理能力。该基准有望更全面地揭示模型在复杂长上下文任务中的真实表现和局限性。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08284v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated rapidly improving long-context capabilities, prompting a wave of benchmarks designed to evaluate them. However, existing long-context evaluations - from Needle-in-a-Haystack (NIAH) tests to more recent multi-hop reasoning and summarization tasks - predominantly measure average-case performance, and many are either saturated or lack robustness. Notably absent is a systematic way to probe how models perform as we scale up the difficulty of tasks along various axes. We address this gap by proposing Pred…
items.why_zh: 该基准提供了系统评估长上下文模型的新方法，有助于发现模型在极端难度下的弱点。
items.why_en: It offers a systematic way to evaluate LLMs on long-context tasks across multiple difficulty axes, revealing weaknesses not captured by average-case benchmarks.

items.title_zh: Blind-Spots-Bench：评估多模态模型中的盲点
items.title_en: Blind-Spots-Bench: Evaluating Blind Spots in Multimodal Models
items.summary_zh: 现代AI模型在众多基准上表现强劲，但在人类看来几乎微不足道的任务上仍会失败，例如操作字符串或画一只五条腿的狗。这表明现有基准可能低估了当前系统的持久盲点。为此，研究者推出了Blind-Spots-Bench，一个旨在通过看似简单但对现代AI仍具挑战的任务来暴露这些盲点的基准。该基准从AI课程学生收集原始问题，经过清洗和标注，形成一套评估集，用于检测多模态模型在常识推理和精细操作等方面的不足。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08317v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern AI models achieve strong performance on many established benchmarks, yet they still fail on tasks that humans find almost trivial, such as manipulating a string or drawing a dog with five legs. These examples suggest that existing benchmarks may under-measure persistent blind spots in current systems. We introduce $\texttt{blind-spots-bench}$, a benchmark designed to expose such blind spots through tasks that appear simple for humans but remain challenging for modern AI. We collect raw questions from students in an AI course, clean and ann…
items.why_zh: 揭示了现有基准未能覆盖的AI盲点，为改进模型鲁棒性提供了重要方向。
items.why_en: It exposes blind spots in AI models that are missed by standard benchmarks, guiding improvements in robustness.

items.title_zh: MobiDiff：面向人类移动数据生成的语义感知多通道离散扩散模型
items.title_en: MobiDiff: Semantic-Aware Multi-Channel Discrete Diffusion for Human Mobility Data Generation
items.summary_zh: 人类移动数据对交通优化、城市规划和资源分配至关重要，但真实数据收集成本高且因隐私问题难以共享。现有扩散方法通常依赖连续或潜在时空轨迹，难以原生建模具有显式区域、活动、时间和间隔结构的离散语义事件。为此，研究者提出MobiDiff，一种语义感知的多通道离散扩散模型，能够生成逼真的移动模式，同时保留语义结构。该方法有望在保护隐私的前提下，为城市计算提供高质量合成数据。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08357v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human mobility data are essential for transportation optimization, urban planning, and resource allocation, yet real-world mobility data are costly to collect and difficult to share due to privacy concerns. Recent diffusion-based methods have shown promise in synthesizing realistic mobility patterns, but they typically rely on continuous or latent spatio-temporal traces, limiting their ability to natively model discrete semantic events with explicit region, activity, time, and interval structures. To address this issue, we introduce MobiDiff, an…
items.why_zh: 提供了一种隐私保护的高质量合成数据生成方法，对城市计算和交通优化具有重要意义。
items.why_en: It enables privacy-preserving generation of realistic mobility data, crucial for urban planning and transportation optimization.

items.title_zh: FedOPAL：基于分析视觉提示调优的单次联邦学习
items.title_en: FedOPAL: One-Shot Federated Learning via Analytic Visual Prompt Tuning
items.summary_zh: 随着基础模型在边缘智能中的广泛部署，通信带宽成为限制联邦学习可扩展性的核心瓶颈。单次联邦学习通过最小化通信轮次缓解了这一问题，但现有迭代微调或知识蒸馏方法仍面临服务器端计算成本高和超参数敏感等挑战。分析联邦学习利用最小二乘闭式解实现高效无梯度聚合，但此前仅适用于线性模型。FedOPAL将分析学习扩展到视觉提示调优，实现单次通信下的高效联邦学习，显著降低通信开销和计算负担。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the widespread deployment of basic models in edge intelligence, communication bandwidth has become a core bottleneck restricting the scalability of federated learning. Although one-shot federated learning alleviates this problem by minimizing communication rounds, existing iterative fine-tuning or knowledge distillation methods still face challenges such as high server-side computational costs and hyperparameter sensitivity. Analytical federated learning achieves efficient gradientfree aggregation using least-squares closed-form solutions, b…
items.why_zh: 大幅降低联邦学习的通信和计算成本，推动基础模型在边缘设备上的实际部署。
items.why_en: It significantly reduces communication and computation costs in federated learning, facilitating deployment of foundation models on edge devices.

items.title_zh: OmniFood-Bench：评估视觉语言模型的营养推理与个性化健康建议
items.title_en: OmniFood-Bench: Evaluating VLMs for Nutrient Reasoning and Personalized Health Advice
items.summary_zh: 大型视觉语言模型（VLM）在个性化医疗和饮食管理中的应用前景广阔，但在食品领域面临“视觉外观与内在营养组成之间的系统性信息不对称”挑战。现有基准主要关注粗粒度分类任务（如食物类别识别），无法评估实际所需的复杂推理链。OmniFood-Bench专门设计用于评估VLM在营养推理和个性化健康建议方面的能力，涵盖从视觉识别到营养分析再到建议生成的完整流程。该基准有望推动VLM在健康领域的可靠应用。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08423v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid integration of Large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) into critical infrastructure promises to revolutionize personalized healthcare and dietary management. However, in the domain of food systems, autonomous agents face a unique and persistent challenge: the "Systemic Information Asymmetry" between visual appearance and intrinsic nutritional composition. Existing benchmarks primarily focus on coarse-grained classification tasks, such as food category recognition, which fail to evaluate the intricate reasoning chain required for real-world…
items.why_zh: 填补了VLM在营养推理评估方面的空白，对推动AI在健康饮食管理中的可靠应用至关重要。
items.why_en: It fills a gap in evaluating VLMs for nutritional reasoning, essential for reliable AI-driven dietary management.

items.title_zh: DATGR：面向生物医学文本的自适应语义建模的漂移感知时序图重连
items.title_en: Drift-Aware Temporal Graph Rewiring (DATGR) for Adaptive Semantic Modeling in Biomedical Text
items.summary_zh: 生物医学语言随着新发现的出现而快速演变，导致传统文本模型随时间失去语义保真度。静态嵌入和共现图无法捕捉这种演变，在检索和知识发现任务中性能下降。DATGR框架通过基于估计的语义漂移动态更新共现边来建模概念演变，无需为每个时间片重新训练嵌入，仅进行轻量级图重连。实验表明，该方法能有效适应生物医学概念的语义漂移，提升下游任务性能。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08490v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Biomedical language evolves rapidly as new discoveries emerge, causing traditional text models to lose semantic fidelity over time. Static embeddings and co-occurrence graphs cannot capture such evolution, leading to performance degradation in retrieval and knowledge discovery tasks. This paper introduces a Drift-Aware Temporal Graph Rewiring (DATGR) framework that models concept evolution by dynamically updating co-occurrence edges based on estimated semantic drift. Instead of retraining embeddings for each time slice, DATGR performs lightweight…
items.why_zh: 提供了一种轻量级方法应对生物医学文本中的语义漂移，对知识发现和检索任务具有重要价值。
items.why_en: It offers a lightweight solution to semantic drift in biomedical texts, improving performance in knowledge discovery and retrieval.

items.title_zh: 自利智能体社会中市场稳定性的形式化机制：市场模拟研究
items.title_en: Formal Mechanisms for Market Stability in Self-Interested Agent Societies: A Marketplace Simulation Study
items.summary_zh: 自利智能体在无约束情况下倾向于在重复社会困境中背叛，导致合作收益崩溃。该研究探讨了在无限制通信之上叠加何种形式化机制足以维持市场稳定，以及这些机制对对抗性攻击的鲁棒性。研究者构建了一个多智能体市场模拟，其中18个LLM智能体（DeepSeek-V3）具有互补生产专长，必须在受约束的市场中交易。实验分析了不同机制（如声誉系统、惩罚机制）对合作和稳定性的影响，为设计稳健的AI经济系统提供了见解。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08652v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-interested agents, left unconstrained, tend toward defection in repeated social dilemmas, causing cooperative gains from trade to collapse. This paper investigates what formal mechanisms, layered on top of unrestricted communication, are sufficient for a society of such agents to maintain market stability, and how resilient those mechanisms are to adversarial attack. We instantiate the research question as a multi-agent marketplace simulation where 18 LLM agents (DeepSeek-V3) with complementary production specialties must trade within a cons…
items.why_zh: 为设计具有合作稳定性的多智能体经济系统提供了理论基础和实验证据。
items.why_en: It provides insights into designing stable multi-agent economic systems by testing formal mechanisms that sustain cooperation.

items.title_zh: 工作流即知识：LLM中介工作流的语义持久化
items.title_en: Workflow as Knowledge: Semantic Persistence for LLM-Mediated Workflows
items.summary_zh: LLM应用越来越多地使用显式工作流来管理工具使用、检索、分支、检查点和人工审批。现有工作流系统已解决许多执行问题，但缺乏对工作流本身作为知识进行语义持久化的关注。该论文提出了一种受Lisp启发但语言无关的概念模型，使用符号形式、对象身份和活图像思维作为解释透镜。在该模型中，工作流定义、实例、推理记录、上下文快照和依赖关系被表示为持久化语义实体，支持工作流的复用、推理和演化。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08740v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) applications increasingly use explicit workflows for tool use, retrieval, branching, checkpointing, and human approval. Existing workflow systems already address many execution concerns. This paper proposes a Lisp-inspired but language-independent conceptual model: symbolic forms, object identity, and live-image thinking are used as explanatory lenses, not implementation commitments. In this model, workflow definitions, workflow instances, inference records, context snapshots, and dependency relations are represented as…
items.why_zh: 将工作流提升为可持久化和推理的知识实体，有助于构建更智能、可复用的LLM应用。
items.why_en: It elevates workflows to persistent knowledge artifacts, enabling smarter and more reusable LLM applications.

items.title_zh: AUTOPILOT VQA：面向行车记录仪事故理解的视觉语言模型基准测试
items.title_en: AUTOPILOT VQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Incident-Centric Dashcam Understanding
items.summary_zh: 近期视觉语言模型、大语言模型和多模态大语言模型的进步提升了自动驾驶任务，如场景理解、决策制定、轨迹预测和视觉问答。然而，评估这些模型能否可靠推理安全关键事故仍具挑战。为此，研究者提出了AUTOPILOT-VQA，一个以事故为中心的视觉问答基准，用于行车记录仪视频理解。该数据集通过结构化问题评估不同系统，旨在填补现有评估空白，推动自动驾驶安全性的提升。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08745v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in Vision-Language Models, Large Language Models, and Multimodal Large Language Models have improved autonomous driving tasks such as scene understanding, decision making, trajectory prediction, and visual question answering. However, evaluating whether these models can reliably reason about safety-critical incidents remains challenging. To address this gap, we present AUTOPILOT-VQA, an incident-centric visual question answering benchmark for dashcam video understanding. The dataset evaluates different systems through structured q…
items.why_zh: 该基准专门针对事故场景，有助于推动自动驾驶系统在安全关键情况下的可靠性评估。
items.why_en: This benchmark focuses on incident scenarios, helping advance reliability evaluation of autonomous driving systems in safety-critical situations.

items.title_zh: 高等教育中AI学习助手的大规模描述性分析
items.title_en: Using AI-based Learning Assistants in Higher Education: A Large-Scale Descriptive Analysis
items.summary_zh: 本研究对高等教育中AI学习助手Syntea的使用进行了大规模描述性分析，基于77,543名远程学习学生的客观日志数据，考察了不同性别、年龄组、学习集群、学位和学习模式下的使用模式。现有教育聊天机器人研究多依赖小样本和自述调查数据，而大规模实际使用行为证据有限。结果显示Syntea已融入学习过程，为AI在教育中的应用提供了实证基础。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08748v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this study, we present a large-scale descriptive analysis of the use of an AI-based learning assistant (Syntea) in higher education. Based on objective log data from 77,543 students enrolled in distance studies, we examine usage patterns across gender, age group, study cluster, degree, and study mode. To date, existing research on educational chatbots has largely relied on comparatively small samples and self-reported survey data, while large-scale evidence on actual usage behavior remains limited. Our findings show that Syntea is already embe…
items.why_zh: 提供了大规模真实使用数据，为AI教育工具的设计和推广提供实证依据。
items.why_en: Provides large-scale real usage data, offering empirical evidence for designing and deploying AI educational tools.

items.title_zh: 思想有基因组：科学谱系推理与基于谱系的创意生成基准
items.title_en: Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation
items.summary_zh: 科学思想很少从零开始，它们继承机制、修复已知局限并重组先前工作，类似生物基因组。当前基准很少评估AI系统能否遵循这种继承结构。研究者提出IdeaGene-Bench（IG-Bench），一个用于科学谱系推理和基于谱系的创意生成基准。该基准围绕IdeaGene框架组织，将每篇论文或提案表示为最小、带类型、有证据支持的思想基因组对象，并评估AI在科学推理和创意生成方面的能力。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08758v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific ideas rarely start from a blank page. They inherit mechanisms, repair known limitations, and recombine pieces of earlier work, much like biological genomes. Current benchmarks still say little about whether AI systems can follow this inheritance structure. We present IdeaGene-Bench (IG-Bench), a benchmark for scientific lineage reasoning and lineage-grounded idea generation. IG-Bench is organized around the IdeaGene framework: each paper or proposal is represented as a set of minimal, typed, evidence-grounded Idea Genome objects, and a…
items.why_zh: 首次系统评估AI在科学谱系推理和创意生成方面的能力，对科学发现自动化有重要意义。
items.why_en: First systematic benchmark for AI's scientific lineage reasoning and idea generation, important for automating scientific discovery.

items.title_zh: 通过记忆回溯和拓扑归因实现时序图网络的可解释性
items.title_en: Towards the Explainability of Temporal Graph Networks via Memory Backtracking and Topological Attribution
items.summary_zh: 时序图在现实应用中普遍存在，时序图网络（TGN）已取得优越预测精度。理解哪些历史事件驱动模型预测可增强TGN的可信度。现有解释方法忽略了记忆模块——记录和更新节点历史的核心组件，未探索过去事件的影响。为此，研究者通过拓扑归因树和记忆回溯树归因TGN预测。拓扑归因树捕捉图结构影响，记忆回溯树追踪历史事件贡献，从而提供更全面的解释。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07716v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Temporal graphs are ubiquitous in real-world applications and Temporal Graph Networks (TGNs) have achieved superior predictive accuracy. Understanding which historical events drive model predictions can enhance trustworthiness of TGNs. Existing explanation methods overlook the memory module, the core component that records and updates node histories, leaving the influence of past events unexplored. To address this, we attribute TGNs predictions through the topology attribution tree and memory backtracking tree. The topology attribution tree cap…
items.why_zh: 首次利用记忆模块解释TGN，提升了时序图模型的可信度和透明度。
items.why_en: First to leverage memory module for TGN explainability, enhancing trust and transparency of temporal graph models.

items.title_zh: ReCoLoRA：面向持续大模型微调的频谱感知递归整合
items.title_en: ReCoLoRA: Spectrum-Aware Recursive Consolidation for Continual LLM Fine-Tuning
items.summary_zh: 参数高效微调可低成本将大语言模型适配到单个任务，但在任务序列中，LoRA类方法不断在相同冻结权重上堆叠低秩更新，导致新任务覆盖旧任务。研究者提出ReCoLoRA（低秩适配器的递归整合），一个频谱感知的持续微调框架：适配器从预训练权重的随机SVD初始化，每层有效秩通过肘部准则选择，主子空间先适配再处理残差。该方法有效缓解了灾难性遗忘，提升了持续学习性能。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07719v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning adapts a large language model to one task cheaply, but across a task sequence LoRA-style methods keep stacking low-rank updates on the same frozen weight, so each new task tends to overwrite the previous ones. We present ReCoLoRA (Recursive Consolidation of Low-Rank Adapters), a spectrum-aware framework for continual fine-tuning: adapters are initialized from a randomized SVD of the pretrained weight, per-layer effective ranks are selected by an elbow criterion, and the principal subspace is adapted before residu…
items.why_zh: 提出频谱感知方法解决持续微调中的灾难性遗忘，对多任务大模型部署有重要价值。
items.why_en: Proposes a spectrum-aware approach to mitigate catastrophic forgetting in continual fine-tuning, valuable for multi-task LLM deployment.

items.title_zh: SHIFT：基于不完整和异质性基因组数据的生存预测
items.title_en: SHIFT: Survival Prediction from Incomplete and Heterogeneous Genomic Data
items.summary_zh: 基因组预测模型常因不同机构的测序面板差异而跨机构迁移失败，导致部署时出现结构性特征缺失。现有方法通常限制分析共享基因、排除不完整患者或依赖测试时插补，这些都会降低鲁棒性并限制多中心数据的使用。研究者提出SHIFT（使用Transformer处理不完整特征的生存预测），一个感知缺失的生存模型，直接处理特征缺失问题，提高了跨机构预测的鲁棒性和泛化能力。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07725v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Genomic prediction models often fail to transfer across institutions because sequencing panels differ across sites, creating structural feature missingness at deployment. Existing approaches to this challenge typically restrict analysis to genes shared across cohorts, exclude patients with incomplete profiles, or rely on test-time imputation, all of which can reduce robustness and limit the use of multi-center data. We propose Survival prediction Handling Incomplete Features using Transformer (SHIFT), a missingness-aware survival model that dir…
items.why_zh: 解决了基因组数据跨机构缺失的难题，提升了生存预测模型的实用性和泛化能力。
items.why_en: Addresses the challenge of missing genomic data across institutions, improving practicality and generalization of survival prediction models.

items.title_zh: 基于基础模型的集体智能
items.title_en: Collective Intelligence with Foundation Models
items.summary_zh: 随着基础模型规模和多样性的增长，协调多个模型形成合作推理系统为更安全、更可靠的AI提供了路径。本章提出一个多智能体框架：求解器模型生成独立草稿，每个草稿经过批评智能体的结构化批评和修订，聚合智能体综合最终共识解决方案。评分模块提供语义、数值和程序性评估。通过微积分、物理、化学等基准的消融研究，展示了该框架在提升推理质量和可靠性方面的有效性。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07729v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: As foundation models grow in scale and diversity, coordinating multiple models into cooperative reasoning systems offers a path toward safer, more reliable AI. This chapter presents a multi-agent framework where solver models generate independent drafts, each undergoes structured critique and revision by a critic agent, and an aggregator agent synthesizes a final consensus solution. A scoring module provides semantic, numerical, and procedural evaluation across all agents. Through ablation studies on a benchmark spanning calculus, physics, chem…
items.why_zh: 展示了多模型协作提升推理质量和可靠性的潜力，为构建安全AI系统提供新思路。
items.why_en: Demonstrates potential of multi-model collaboration for improved reasoning and reliability, offering new insights for building safe AI systems.

items.title_zh: MetaNCA实现架构泛化
items.title_en: Architecture Generalization with MetaNCA
items.summary_zh: 自组织是生命涌现的特性，由个体组件基于局部信息的集体行为驱动。生物神经元通过突触的局部交互高效学习，并在生命周期内适应连接。受这些理想特性启发，神经细胞自动机（NCA）模型已成功仅通过局部更新规则学习形态发生，并在多次更新后保持稳定。本文提出MetaNCA，旨在提升NCA的架构泛化能力，使其能适应不同网络结构和任务。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07743v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Self-organization is an emergent property of life, driven by the collective behavior of individual components acting on local information. Biological neurons, through local interactions transmitted through synapses, are able to learn efficiently and can adapt their connections over an organism's lifespan. Motivated by these desirable properties of adaptability and local interaction, neural cellular automata (NCA) models have been successful at learning morphogenesis solely through local update rules, demonstrating stability over many updates an…
items.why_zh: 提升NCA的架构泛化能力，有助于开发更灵活、自适应的AI系统。
items.why_en: Improving architecture generalization of NCA contributes to developing more flexible and adaptive AI systems.

items.title_zh: 深度强化学习评估与设计范式的原则性分析
items.title_en: Principled Analysis of Deep Reinforcement Learning Evaluation and Design Paradigms
items.summary_zh: 本文对深度强化学习（DRL）研究中的关键评估与设计范式进行了原则性分析。自深度神经网络用于近似状态-动作价值函数并攻克极具挑战性的游戏以来，强化学习领域取得了显著进展。论文聚焦于推动这一进展的核心要素，系统分析了当前DRL研究中常用的评估方法和设计模式，旨在为未来研究提供更严谨的指导。该工作有助于提升DRL研究的可重复性和可靠性。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07769v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Starting from the utilization of deep neural networks to approximate the state-action value function that led to winning one of the most challenging games, to algorithmic advancements that allowed solving problems without even explicitly stating the rules of the challenge at hand, reinforcement learning research has been the center of remarkable scientific progress for the past decade. In this paper, we focus on the key ingredients of this research progress and we analyze the canonical evaluation and design paradigms in reinforcement learning.…
items.why_zh: 该分析有助于规范深度强化学习的研究方法，提升实验评估的严谨性。
items.why_en: This analysis helps standardize DRL research methodologies and improve the rigor of experimental evaluations.

items.title_zh: 从求解器到研究：大语言模型驱动的形式化数学迈向研究前沿
items.title_en: From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier
items.summary_zh: 近年来，AI for Mathematics（AI4Math）领域，特别是大语言模型（LLM）驱动的定理证明器，在通过交互式定理证明（ITP）语言生成形式化证明方面取得了显著成功。然而，当前系统在处理前沿研究数学（如发现新定理或解决开放猜想）时仍存在根本性局限，这些问题通常是开放式的、定义不明确的，并涉及多层抽象。本文论证了AI4Math的下一个飞跃需要超越现有求解器范式，向能够处理研究级数学挑战的系统迈进。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07779v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Recent developments in AI for Mathematics (AI4Math), especially Large Language Model (LLM)-driven theorem provers, has achieved remarkable success in formal proof generation for well-defined mathematical problems through Interactive Theorem Proving (ITP) languages. However, current systems remain fundamentally limited in tackling frontier research mathematics, such as discovering new theorems or resolving open conjectures, which are often open-ended, under-specified, and involve multiple layers of abstraction. We argue that the next leap in AI4…
items.why_zh: 该研究指明了AI for Mathematics从解题工具向科研助手演进的关键方向。
items.why_en: This work points to a key direction for AI4Math to evolve from problem-solving tools to research assistants.

items.title_zh: 从触发到情绪：基于CPM的评估多智能体实现角色对话中的动态情感演化
items.title_en: From Triggers to Emotions: A CPM-Grounded Appraisal Multi-Agent for Dynamic Emotional Evolution in Persona-Based Dialogue
items.summary_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）显著推动了基于角色的对话智能体在医疗、教育、咨询、客服和互动叙事等领域的应用。然而，现有工作存在关键缺口：基于角色的对话系统通常将情绪编码为静态特征或表面风格线索，而情感对话研究主要关注对用户的共情回应，而非建模智能体自身的情感演变。本文提出一种基于认知评价理论（CPM）的多智能体框架，使对话智能体能够根据事件触发动态演化自身情绪状态，从而生成更真实、连贯的角色互动。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07824v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have substantially advanced persona-based dialogue agents for emotion-sensitive role simulation in healthcare, education, counseling, customer service, and interactive storytelling. However, two related lines of work leave a key gap. Persona-based dialogue systems often encode emotions as static traits or surface-level stylistic cues, and affective dialogue research has largely focused on empathetic response generation toward users rather than modeling the agent persona's own evolving emotional state. As a result, t…
items.why_zh: 该研究填补了对话智能体自身情感动态建模的空白，提升了角色扮演的真实性。
items.why_en: This research fills the gap in modeling agents' own emotional dynamics, enhancing realism in persona-based dialogue.

items.title_zh: Shift & Drift：面向泛化与鲁棒自动驾驶运动规划的零样本基准
items.title_en: Shift & Drift: A Zero-Shot Benchmark for Generalizable and Robust Autonomous Driving Motion Planning
items.summary_zh: 尽管在nuPlan等大规模数据集上训练的闭环运动规划器在分布内（ID）场景中表现强劲，但其对新型城市拓扑结构的泛化能力以及执行扰动后的恢复机制仍研究不足。为此，本文提出Shift & Drift，一个双轨基准测试，旨在严格测试运动规划器在两种分布偏移下的表现：语义偏移轨道利用新型转换流水线将航拍深度场景数据转换为规划输入；漂移轨道则评估规划器在遭遇执行扰动后的恢复能力。该基准为评估自动驾驶规划器的鲁棒性和泛化性提供了标准化测试平台。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07844v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: While closed-loop motion planners trained on large-scale, object-level datasets, e.g., nuPlan, demonstrate strong in-distribution (ID) performance, their generalization to novel urban topologies and recovery mechanisms following execution perturbations remain under-explored. To address this, we present Shift & Drift, a novel dual-track benchmark designed to rigorously stress-test motion planners across two critical axes of distribution shift: (1) The Semantic Shift Track leverages a novel conversion pipeline that transforms the aerial, DeepScen…
items.why_zh: 该基准填补了自动驾驶运动规划在分布外场景下评估的空白，有助于推动更鲁棒的规划算法。
items.why_en: This benchmark addresses the lack of out-of-distribution evaluation for motion planning, promoting more robust algorithms.

items.title_zh: 张量网络理论的多智能体自动形式化
items.title_en: Multi-agent Autoformalization of Tensor Network Theory
items.summary_zh: 本文构建了一个由专门的大语言模型智能体组成的团队，并提出了一个智能体驱动的工作流，用于理论物理研究层面的形式化验证。以矩阵乘积态基本定理的自动形式化为演示案例，这些智能体通过结构化的数学蓝图和定期人工审查进行协调，自主编排并执行了完整的形式化过程。对于某些陈述，智能体甚至探索了标准文献中未包含的新证明路径。该工作展示了多智能体系统在复杂科学形式化中的潜力。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07857v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We build a team of specialized large language-model agents and present an agent-driven workflow for research-level formalization in theoretical physics, with the autoformalization of the fundamental theorem of matrix-product states as a demonstration. The agents, coordinated through a structured mathematical blueprint and periodic human review, orchestrated and executed the full formalization autonomously. For some statements, the agents were able to explore new proof routes that are not part of the standard literature. Along the way the agents…
items.why_zh: 该研究展示了多智能体系统在自动形式化复杂科学理论方面的能力，有望加速数学和物理的形式化验证。
items.why_en: This work showcases the capability of multi-agent systems in autoformalizing complex scientific theories, potentially accelerating formal verification in mathematics and physics.

items.title_zh: 跨视觉、语言、视频和音频的多模态遗忘：方法、数据集与基准综述
items.title_en: Multimodal Unlearning Across Vision, Language, Video, and Audio: Survey of Methods, Datasets, and Benchmarks
items.summary_zh: 随着视觉语言模型（VLM）、扩散模型（DM）、大语言模型（LLM）和音频基础模型（AFM）的广泛应用，这些多模态基础模型可能无意中编码了训练数据中的敏感、版权、偏见或不安全的跨模态关联。在删除请求或政策更新后重新训练往往不切实际，而针对性地遗忘因知识分布在共享表示中而变得困难。多模态遗忘通过实现跨模态的选择性移除同时保持整体效用，解决了这一挑战。本文全面综述了多模态遗忘的方法、数据集和基准，为该领域提供了系统性的参考。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07907v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: With the growing adoption of VLMs, DMs, LLMs, and AFMs, these multimodal foundation models can inadvertently encode sensitive, copyrighted, biased, or unsafe cross-modal associations that originate from their training data. Retraining after deletion requests or policy updates is often impractical, and targeted forgetting remains difficult because knowledge is distributed across shared representations. Multimodal unlearning addresses this challenge by enabling selective removal across modalities while retaining overall utility. This survey offer…
items.why_zh: 该综述系统梳理了多模态遗忘领域的研究现状，对于构建安全、合规的多模态AI系统具有重要参考价值。
items.why_en: This survey systematically reviews the state of multimodal unlearning, offering valuable insights for building safe and compliant multimodal AI systems.

items.title_zh: 通过潜在人格特质实现语言模型的高效安全对齐
items.title_en: Efficient Safety Alignment of Language Models via Latent Personality Traits
items.summary_zh: 当前大语言模型的安全方法易受对抗攻击，促使研究者探索鲁棒替代方案。潜在对抗训练（LAT）是其中有效的防御手段之一，但会降低模型效用，且需要在大量有害提示数据集上训练。本文提出潜在人格对齐（LPA），该方法仅使用从心理测量人格文献中提取的66条与危害无关的陈述进行对抗训练，取代了显式的拒绝有害行为。实验表明，LPA在保持模型效用的同时，有效提升了对抗鲁棒性，为语言模型安全对齐提供了一种高效且轻量的新思路。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07918v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Current safety methods for large language models are known to be vulnerable to adversarial attacks, motivating research into robust alternatives. Latent Adversarial Training (LAT) is among the most effective defenses, but can degrade utility and requires training on large datasets of harmful prompts. We introduce Latent Personality Alignment (LPA), which replaces explicit harm refusal with adversarial training on just 66 harm-agnostic statements drawn from psychometric personality literature. We hypothesize that personality-anchored representat…
items.why_zh: LPA以极小的数据需求实现了高效的安全对齐，为语言模型的安全防护提供了轻量级新方案。
items.why_en: LPA achieves efficient safety alignment with minimal data, providing a lightweight solution for language model safety.

items.title_zh: path_boost：基于路径梯度提升的可解释图级预测Python包
items.title_en: path_boost: A Python Package for Interpretable Graph-Level Prediction using Path-Based Gradient Boosting
items.summary_zh: 本文介绍了path_boost，一个用于图结构输入数据可解释监督学习的Python包。该包实现了PathBoost算法，一种梯度提升方法，能够在学习过程中自动发现图中具有预测性的标记路径。与通常难以解释的图神经网络不同，PathBoost生成基于路径特征的加性预测模型，明确揭示哪些子结构驱动了预测。为避免穷举所有可能路径，算法采用高效搜索策略。该工具为图级预测任务提供了可解释性强的解决方案。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07935v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We present path_boost, a Python package for interpretable supervised learning on graph-structured input data. The package implements PathBoost, a gradient boosting algorithm that automatically discovers predictive labeled paths within graphs during the learning process. Unlike graph neural networks, which are generally difficult to interpret, PathBoost produces an additive prediction model over path-based features that explicitly reveals which substructures drive predictions. To avoid an exhaustive enumeration of all possible paths, the algorit…
items.why_zh: 该工具为图级预测提供了可解释的替代方案，有助于理解模型决策背后的图结构特征。
items.why_en: This tool offers an interpretable alternative for graph-level prediction, helping to understand the graph substructures driving model decisions.

items.title_zh: 线性注意力架构：机制、权衡与跨层路由
items.title_en: Linear Attention Architectures: Mechanisms, Trade-offs, and Cross-Layer Routing
items.summary_zh: 本文对softmax注意力与四种近期提出的循环线性注意力架构（DeltaNet、Gated DeltaNet、Kimi Delta Attention和Gated DeltaNet-2）进行了比较研究。这些架构通过统一的循环记忆表示法进行描述，明确揭示了它们在表达能力、记忆衰减、擦除与写入控制、训练吞吐量以及实现复杂度等方面的差异。该研究有助于理解线性注意力机制在长序列处理中的优势与局限，为未来高效注意力模型的设计提供指导。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07953v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Self-attention lets each token retrieve information from the full context, but its quadratic cost in sequence length limits training and inference at long context. This paper presents a comparative study of softmax attention and four recent recurrent linear-attention architectures: DeltaNet, Gated DeltaNet, Kimi Delta Attention, and Gated DeltaNet-2. We express these mechanisms in a common recurrent-memory notation, making explicit how they differ in expressivity, memory decay, erase and write control, training throughput, and implementation co…
items.why_zh: 线性注意力是解决Transformer长序列计算瓶颈的关键方向，该比较研究为选择或设计高效注意力架构提供了系统参考。
items.why_en: Linear attention is key to addressing the quadratic cost of Transformers for long sequences; this comparative study provides a systematic reference for selecting or designing efficient attention architectures.

items.title_zh: 谁破坏了系统？基于LLM的多智能体系统中的故障定位
items.title_en: Who Broke the System? Failure Localization in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
items.summary_zh: 基于大语言模型的多智能体系统通过协调推理与行动实现复杂问题求解，但其分布式结构也带来了系统级故障诊断的新挑战。当执行失败时，由于长程交互和智能体行为的紧密耦合，难以确定哪个智能体应负责以及轨迹在何时开始不可逆地偏离。本文研究了基于LLM的多智能体系统中的故障定位问题，提出了相应方法。该工作对于提升多智能体系统的可靠性和可调试性具有重要意义。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.07989v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Large language model (LLM) based multi-agent systems enable complex problem solving through coordinated reasoning and action, but their distributed structure also introduces new challenges in diagnosing system-level failures. When an execution fails, identifying which agent is responsible and at what point the trajectory first becomes irreversibly misdirected is difficult due to long-horizon interactions and tightly coupled agent behaviors. In this paper, we study the problem of failure localization in LLM-based multi-agent systems and present…
items.why_zh: 多智能体系统故障定位是确保其可靠运行的关键，该研究填补了该领域的方法空白。
items.why_en: Failure localization is crucial for reliable multi-agent systems; this research fills a methodological gap in the field.

items.title_zh: 我们能信任LLM的逻辑吗？基于图框架量化不确定性、一致性与鲁棒性
items.title_en: Can We Trust LLM's Logic? Quantifying Uncertainty, Coherence, and Robustness via a Graph-Based Framework
items.summary_zh: 大语言模型容易产生有缺陷和不忠实的推理，而自一致性等解码策略仅评估最终答案的一致性，忽略了中间步骤的逻辑有效性。本文提出三个基本问题：如何可靠地量化LLM推理中的不确定性？语义、结构和因果意识能否比朴素多数投票选择更忠实的推理？推理拓扑在对抗条件下的鲁棒性如何？为此，作者提出了一个基于图的框架来量化LLM推理的不确定性、一致性和鲁棒性。该研究为评估和改进LLM推理的可信度提供了新工具。
items.summary_en: arXiv:2607.08017v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Large-Language Models (LLMs) can be prone to flawed and unfaithful reasoning that decoding strategies like Self-Consistency (SC) fail to detect as they evaluate only final-answer agreement while ignoring the logical validity of intermediate steps. This raises three fundamental questions: How can we reliably quantify uncertainty in LLM reasoning? Can semantic, structural, and causal awareness select more faithful reasoning compared to na\"ive majority voting? and How robust is reasoning topology under adversarial conditions? To address these que…
items.why_zh: LLM推理的可信度是实际应用的核心问题，该框架为系统评估推理质量提供了新方法。
items.why_en: Trustworthiness of LLM reasoning is central to real-world applications; this framework offers a systematic method for evaluating reasoning quality.
